title: Patron seals
preface: A seal is a sponsored mark that funds a chamber, route, or tool without claiming to own the canon. Empty pedestals remain visible so the affordance is honest.
policy: Seals must remain legible as patronage—never disguised as neutral narration.
Sponsored routes keep provenance: who funded, what was funded, when the stone was set.
Editorial judgment stays with the scriptorium; a seal buys presence, not rewrite rights.
Paid links appear as marked thresholds, not hidden doors.

seals.label: Open pedestal
seals.blurb: A chamber, motif bed, or tool route can carry a patron mark here.

seals.label: Open pedestal
seals.blurb: Reserved for a mark that funds work the audience already wants to return to.

seals.label: Example · Atelier mark
seals.blurb: Illustrative only: a quiet mark linking to the craftsperson who raises neighboring monuments.
